title: India Independent
description: India Independent is a book by Charles Bettelheim. This book cannot be imported into India.
descriptionParagraphs: India Independent is a book by Charles Bettelheim. This book cannot be imported into India.

What makes it interesting is the way a book becomes legible to officials as a political instrument rather than a neutral cultural object. As a book, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in India. The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: This book cannot be imported into India. It was banned for criticising the policies of the Indian government. More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

overview.englishDescription: India Independent is a book by Charles Bettelheim. This book cannot be imported into India.
banningParagraphs: India Independent entered censorship debates as a book associated with politics, public argument, and state power. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around political control and political dissent.

The earliest event currently captured here is 1976 in India, where Government of India or British Indian authorities banned publication, sale, or possession. This book cannot be imported into India. It was banned for criticising the policies of the Indian government. This book cannot be imported into India. It was banned for criticising the policies of the Indian government.

counterReadings.title: The Origins of Totalitarianism
counterReadings.author: Hannah Arendt
counterReadings.note: A foundational analysis of state terror, propaganda, and ideological conformity.

counterReadings.title: On Tyranny
counterReadings.author: Timothy Snyder
counterReadings.note: A short modern guide to resisting authoritarian politics and controlled public discourse.

counterReadings.title: 100 Banned Books: Censorship Histories of World Literature
counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: A compact reference on how censorship systems moved across states, churches, and courts.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: 387 B.C. to 1978 A.D.
counterReadings.author: Anne Lyon Haight
counterReadings.note: Useful for comparing older obscenity, heresy, and political bans with modern free-speech disputes.
